what_changed: Provider-side imported literal export safety now admits existing free-name collection factory calls only through their admitted LibraryApi occurrence proof.
Provider-side imported literal export safety now admits existing imported-binding collection factory calls only through their admitted LibraryApi occurrence proof.

Provider-side imported literal export safety now admits existing Java collection factory calls only through their admitted LibraryApi occurrence proof, exact-safe literal arguments, and static-null policy for null-rejecting immutable factories.

The shared export-safety helper also reuses existing Ruby Set.new occurrence admission, but Ruby module-import snapshots are not counted in the product delta because the module-import resolver does not currently route Ruby provider/importer pairs.

Missing LibraryApi evidence, provider-local factory shadowing, provider/importer mutations, wrong contents, ambiguous single-argument Arrays.asList(...) providers, and raw import-coordinate sequences remain closed.

notes: Missing Java collection-factory LibraryApi evidence blocks snapshots.
Ambiguous single-argument Arrays.asList(...) provider snapshots stay closed.
Missing Python builtin collection-factory LibraryApi evidence blocks snapshots.
Missing Python stdlib imported collection-factory LibraryApi evidence blocks snapshots.
Provider-local Python builtin factory shadowing blocks snapshots.
The full crates recall-loss report did not move oracle completeness because the new capability is pinned by focused imported-value fixtures rather than an existing crates corpus under-merge.

The +1 admission rejection comes from additional test/helper units introduced by this change; it lands in the structured import-symbol callee-identity bucket, with no new top opportunities and no hard-gate movement.
This phase strengthens the semantic kernel by reusing existing LibraryApi collection-factory capabilities across the import-snapshot boundary instead of adding selector-specific API exceptions.
decision.follow_up: Phase 3 should add import-snapshot miss-reason instrumentation and corpus census reporting, so future support expansion is driven by observable provider/export blockers rather than hand-picked language surfaces.
